text: OWASP A03:2021 Injection. Injection flaws occur when untrusted data is sent to an interpreter as part of a command or query. SQL injection, NoSQL injection, OS command injection, and LDAP injection are common. Prevention: use parameterized queries, ORMs, input validation, and least privilege database accounts.

OWASP A07:2021 Cross-Site Scripting (XSS). XSS flaws occur whenever an application includes untrusted data in a web page without proper validation or escaping. Use context-aware output encoding, Content Security Policy, and frameworks that auto-escape by default.

CWE-89 Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection'). The application constructs SQL commands using externally-influenced input without neutralizing special elements. Mitigation: prepared statements and parameterized queries.

CVE-2023-1234 (sample): A web application fails to sanitize user input in a database query, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary SQL. Affected components include login forms using string concatenation. Upgrade to parameterized queries and apply WAF rules.
